message.content:
你是调研 finder。任务背景:MAformac 项目(纯端侧 macOS SwiftUI 演示助手,主演示面已定为 macOS,后续要做 UIUE 前置化)。
要求:
- 先用 ToolSearch 加载 WebSearch 和 WebFetch,然后联网搜索。
- 每条 findings 的 claim 必须来自你真实打开(WebFetch)或搜索摘要确认过的来源;禁止凭记忆编造 URL、数字、版本号。能 WebFetch 的关键来源尽量实访,并把 fetched 标 true。
- 优先 2024-2026 的新资料(Liquid Glass / macOS 26 时代),老经典(HIG 长期原则、Nielsen 启发式)也可收但注明。
- 输出全部用中文(URL/API 名/专有名词保留英文)。
- 8-15 条高质量 findings,宁缺毋滥。
调研角度:可用性与 UIUE 评审——Nielsen 启发式评估及桌面端应用、macOS 特有交互质量维度(键盘快捷键、菜单栏、拖拽、多窗口、指针悬停)、可访问性(VoiceOver/键盘导航/Dynamic Type/对比度)验收方法、UI 走查(design review/UI audit)的 checklist 化方法、demo 场景下"惊艳感"的评估维度。

attachment.content:
- archive-research-pack: Use when 一次多路 workflow 调研 / deep-research / pre-mortem oracle 搜证收口,需要把成果落盘防失忆。症状:调研完只想存一个总报告、transcript 在 /tmp 待清会丢、finder 引的 source URL 事后无法复核、各路一手档与综合报告混在一起。
- axiom-accessibility: Use when fixing or auditing ANY accessibility issue — VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, color contrast, touch targets, WCAG compliance, App Store accessibility review.
- axiom-ai: Use when implementing ANY Apple Intelligence or on-device AI feature. Covers Foundation Models, @Generable, LanguageModelSession, structured output, Tool protocol, iOS 26 AI integration.
- axiom-apple-docs: Use when ANY question involves Apple framework APIs, Swift compiler errors, or Xcode-bundled documentation. Covers Liquid Glass, Swift 6.2 concurrency, Foundation Models, SwiftData, StoreKit, 32 Swift compiler diagnostics.
- axiom-build: Use when ANY iOS build fails, test crashes, Xcode misbehaves, or environment issue occurs before debugging code. Covers build failures, compilation errors, dependency conflicts, simulator problems, environment-first diagnostics.
- axiom-concurrency: Use when writing ANY async code, actors, threads, or seeing ANY concurrency error. Covers Swift 6 concurrency, @MainActor, Sendable, data races, async/await patterns.
- axiom-data: Use when working with ANY data persistence, database, storage, CloudKit, migration, or serialization. Covers SwiftData, Core Data, GRDB, SQLite, CloudKit sync, file storage, Codable, migrations.
- axiom-design: Use when making design decisions, implementing HIG patterns, Liquid Glass, SF Symbols, typography, or structuring app entry points and authentication flows.
- axiom-games: Use when building ANY 2D or 3D game with SpriteKit, SceneKit, or RealityKit, or adding touch controls or game controller support. Covers scene graphs, ECS, physics, actions, game loops, rendering, TouchController, GCController.
- axiom-graphics: Use when working with ANY GPU rendering, Metal, OpenGL migration, shaders, 3D content, RealityKit, AR, USD/USDZ files, or display performance. Covers Metal migration, shader conversion, RealityKit ECS, RealityView, USDKit, ProMotion.
- axiom-health: Use when working with HealthKit, WorkoutKit, health data, workouts, or fitness features on iOS or watchOS. Covers permissions, queries, background delivery, custom workouts, multidevice coordination.
- axiom-integration: Use when integrating ANY iOS system feature - Siri, Shortcuts, widgets, IAP, localization, privacy, alarms, calendar, reminders, contacts, background tasks, push notifications, timers. Covers App Intents, WidgetKit, StoreKit, EventKit, Contacts.
- axiom-location: Use when implementing location services, maps, geofencing, or debugging location/MapKit issues. Covers Core Location, CLMonitor, MapKit, annotations, directions.
- axiom-macos: Use when building ANY macOS app — windows, menus, sandboxing, distribution, AppKit bridging or modernization (control events, state restoration, concentric corners), or macOS-specific SwiftUI patterns.
- axiom-media: Use when working with camera, photos, audio, haptics, ShazamKit, or Now Playing. Covers AVCaptureSession, PHPicker, PhotosPicker, AVFoundation, Core Haptics, audio recognition, MediaPlayer, CarPlay, MusicKit.
- axiom-networking: Use when implementing or debugging ANY network connection, API call, or socket. Covers URLSession, Network.framework, NetworkConnection, connection diagnostics.
- axiom-payments: Use when accepting ANY real-world payment — Apple Pay, Wallet passes, Tap to Pay, Orders in Wallet. NOT in-app purchase / digital content (use axiom-integration). Covers entitlements, certs, signing, App Review payment rules.
- axiom-performance: Use when app feels slow, memory grows, battery drains, or diagnosing ANY performance issue. Covers memory leaks, profiling, Instruments workflows, retain cycles, performance optimization.
- axiom-security: Use when storing credentials securely, encrypting data, implementing passkeys, securing AI/agentic features against prompt injection, code signing, or managing certificates and provisioning profiles.
- axiom-shipping: Use when preparing ANY app for submission, handling App Store rejections, writing appeals, or managing App Store Connect. Covers submission checklists, rejection troubleshooting, metadata requirements, privacy manifests, age ratings, export compliance.
- axiom-swift: Use when reviewing Swift code for modern idioms, working with noncopyable types, implementing drag and drop, adding debug deep links, or building for tvOS.
- axiom-swiftui: Use when building, fixing, or improving ANY SwiftUI UI — views, navigation, layout, animations, performance, architecture, gestures, debugging, iOS 26 features.
- axiom-testing: Use when writing ANY test, debugging flaky tests, making tests faster, or choosing Swift Testing vs XCTest. Covers unit tests, UI tests, async testing, test architecture.
- axiom-tools: Use when asking how to use Axiom or what skills exist, capturing console with xclog, symbolicating .ips/MetricKit/.crash crashes with xcsym, driving/validating simulator UI & accessibility with xcui, or analyzing xctrace/CPU profiles with xcprof.
- axiom-uikit: Use when bridging UIKit and SwiftUI, modernizing UIKit apps (scene lifecycle, resizability), debugging Auto Layout, Combine, TextKit, PencilKit, or UIKit animations.
- axiom-vision: Use when implementing ANY computer vision feature — image analysis, pose detection, person segmentation, subject lifting, text recognition, barcode scanning.
- axiom-watchos: Use when building ANY watchOS app — app structure, independent apps, Watch Connectivity, Smart Stack widgets, complications, controls, RelevanceKit, background tasks, ClockKit migration.
- axiom-xcode-mcp: Use when connecting to Xcode via MCP, using xcrun mcpbridge, or working with ANY Xcode MCP tool (XcodeRead, BuildProject, RunTests, RenderPreview). Covers setup, tool reference, workflow patterns, troubleshooting.
- closeout-receipt-writer: Use when 一个 apply/重型任务/审计 wave 收口需要写 receipt 证据。症状:想用 hardcoded pass、读 metadata flag 自证 verdict、凭印象写状态、或 receipt 与实跑 log 脱节;0-34 灾难根因正是 receipt 读自己的 metadata flag 翻 pass、零 token 证据。
- doc-cascade-sweep: Use when 范式翻案/架构大改/口径回写后,grep 全仓命中几十个文件含过期锚点或旧数字,需要全仓级联更新。症状:看到 grep 命中 60+ 文件就想 sed 全量替换、或一个决策只 append 进一个文件没回写文档组、或改了带日期的历史快照正文破坏一手溯源。
- ios-debugger-agent: Build, run, and debug iOS apps on Simulator with XcodeBuildMCP. Use when launching an app, inspecting simulator UI or logs, or diagnosing runtime behavior.
- ios-ettrace-performance: Capture and interpret iOS Simulator ETTrace profiles. Use when profiling launch or runtime latency, comparing traces, or finding CPU-heavy stacks.
- ios-simulator-skill: 29 production-ready scripts for iOS app testing, building, and automation. Provides semantic UI navigation, build automation, accessibility testing, and simulator lifecycle management. Optimized for AI agents with minimal token output.
- openspec-apply-change: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Use when the user wants to start implementing, continue implementation, or work through tasks.
- openspec-archive-change: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow. Use when the user wants to finalize and archive a change after implementation is complete.
- openspec-explore: Enter explore mode - a thinking partner for exploring ideas, investigating problems, and clarifying requirements. Use when the user wants to think through something before or during a change.
- openspec-propose: Propose a new change with all artifacts generated in one step. Use when the user wants to quickly describe what they want to build and get a complete proposal with design, specs, and tasks ready for implementation.
- openspec-sync-specs: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs. Use when the user wants to update main specs with changes from a delta spec, without archiving the change.
- verify-external-claims: Use when 一个多路 workflow/finder 调研、deep-research、或综合官报告引用了 arxiv ID、精确数字（调用数/百分比/star/benchmark）、或 repo 活跃度断言,且这些要驱动决策。症状:finder 在搜证压力下高发编造精确数字 + 引用 ID,且常安到真实存在的 source 上(issue/论文真、数字假),综合官即使做了本机 grep 也漏外部声称。
- opsx:apply: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change (Experimental)
- opsx:archive: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow
- opsx:explore: Enter explore mode - think through ideas, investigate problems, clarify requirements
- opsx:propose: Propose a new change - create it and generate all artifacts in one step
- opsx:sync: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Web search results for query: "Nielsen 10 usability heuristics desktop application evaluation 2024"

Links: [{"title":"Nielsen’s 10 Usability Heuristics | by Maggie Lin | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@zero_guess_one/nielsens-10-usability-heuristics-d19c426bf8c5"},{"title":"How I Developed the 10 Usability Heuristics","url":"https://jakobnielsenphd.substack.com/p/usability-heuristics-history"},{"title":"10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design - NN/G","url":"https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/"},{"title":"Jakob Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics: definitions and examples","url":"https://blog-ux.com/en/jakob-nielsen-s-10-usability-heuristics/"},{"title":"Usability Principles – Jakob Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design - UX24/7","url":"https://ux247.com/usability-principles/"},{"title":"Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics | Heurio","url":"https://www.heurio.co/nielsens-10-usability-heuristics"},{"title":"Nielson’s 10 Usability Heuristics for UI Design | UXtweak","url":"https://blog.uxtweak.com/usability-heuristics/"},{"title":"Investigating usability of mobile health applications in Bangladesh","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2004.07044"},{"title":"Adapting Nielsen's Usability Heuristics to the Context of Mobile   Augmented Reality","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.03174"},{"title":"Nielsen’s Heuristics: 10 Usability Principles To Improve UI Design | by Nasir Ahmed | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@nasir-ahmed03/nielsens-heuristics-10-usability-principles-to-improve-ui-design-0ed7d78011f8"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics and their application to desktop application evaluation:

## Overview

Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics were first published by Jakob Nielsen in 1994 and remain the most widely used checklist in UX design today. 2024 marks the 30-year anniversary of the 10 usability heuristics.

## Application to Desktop Applications

Nielsen's 10 heuristics work well for reviewing general-purpose product interfaces including SaaS apps, dashboards, and other applications, and they work equally well for desktop, mobile, and web.

## Heuristic Evaluation Method

Heuristic evaluation against Nielsen's 10 is the default method for surfacing usability issues without running a full user test, where two to five reviewers walk through the product, flag each violation, and rate its severity. In practice, three evaluators tend to catch around 60% of the usability issues a wider study would find — at a fraction of the cost.

## Enduring Relevance

While the language of the definitions has been slightly refined, the 10 heuristics themselves have remained relevant and unchanged since 1994, and when something has remained true for 26 years, it will likely apply to future generations of user interfaces as well.

Links: [{"title":"macos-design-guidelines | Skills Mar...","url":"https://lobehub.com/skills/bsreeram08-chowser-macos-design-guidelines"},{"title":"Menu Anatomy - Menus - macOS - Human Interface Guidelines - Apple Developer","url":"https://developers.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/menus/menu-anatomy/"},{"title":"User Interaction - Mac Catalyst - Human Interface Guidelines - Apple Developer","url":"https://developers.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/mac-catalyst/overview/user-interaction/"},{"title":"macos-design-guidelines — skills.re","url":"https://skills.re/skills/ehmo/platform-design-skills/macos-design-guidelines"},{"title":"Drag and Drop - User Interaction - macOS - Human Interface Guidelines - Apple Developer","url":"https://developers.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/user-interaction/drag-and-drop/"},{"title":"Apple Human Interface Guidelines (HIG) · GitHub","url":"https://gist.github.com/eonist/f4ba31012815731284d867232f6c70e4"},{"title":"Apple Human Interface Guidelines: Menus","url":"https://leopard-adc.pepas.com/documentation/UserExperience/Conceptual/AppleHIGuidelines/XHIGMenus/XHIGMenus.html"},{"title":"The menu bar - System experiences - Components - Human Interface Guidelines - Design - Apple Developer","url":"https://developers.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/components/system-experiences/the-menu-bar/"},{"title":"Apple Human Interface Guidelines: Controls","url":"https://leopard-adc.pepas.com/documentation/UserExperience/Conceptual/AppleHIGuidelines/XHIGControls/XHIGControls.html"},{"title":"Human Interface Guidelines | Apple Developer Documentation","url":"https://developer.apple.com/macos/human-interface-guidelines/user-interaction/drag-and-drop/"}]

I found search results related to the macOS Human Interface Guidelines covering the topics you requested. Here's what the current guidance covers:

## Keyboard Shortcuts

Consider assigning keyboard shortcuts to frequently used menu items in the menu bar, like Command-C for Copy, which lets people quickly invoke the menu item anytime using a simple keystroke. People expect to use keyboard shortcuts they already know for standard menu items, like Copy, Cut, Paste, Save, and Print, and developers should define custom keyboard shortcuts only when necessary.

## Menu Bar

Developers should enable system-defined menus and menu items that are relevant in their app, as people generally expect to find familiar menus and menu items in the apps and games they use, and in many cases the system implements the functionality of standard menu items. Using the default ordering of menu bar menus and menu items within each menu makes it easier for people to find what they're looking for.

## Drag and Drop

Drag and drop should be supported for content manipulation—reordering items, moving between containers, importing files from Finder, and exporting content—because Mac is a drag-and-drop platform and if users can see content, they expect to drag it somewhere. Display a pointer that indicates what happens when content is dropped, such as the copy pointer when dragging a new icon into a toolbar, or other pointers like drag link, disappearing item, and operation not allowed.

## Pointer

Mac is a pointer-driven platform where every interactive element must respond to hover, click, right-click, and drag, and all interactive elements must have a visible hover state.

Links: [{"title":"Liquid Glass in Swift: Official Best Practices for iOS 26 & macOS Tahoe - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/diskcleankit/liquid-glass-in-swift-official-best-practices-for-ios-26-macos-tahoe-1coo"},{"title":"Merlin Project | The Ultimate iOS 26 & macOS Tahoe Guide","url":"https://www.projectwizards.net/en/blog/2025/09/macos26-ios26-tips"},{"title":"How to Customize Your Mac with macOS Tahoe 26’s Liquid Glass Design","url":"https://www.itech4mac.net/2025/07/how-to-customize-macos-tahoe-26s-liquid-glass-design/"},{"title":"macOS Tahoe: Everything We Know | MacRumors","url":"https://www.macrumors.com/roundup/macos-26/"},{"title":"Liquid Glass, macOS Tahoe, macOS 26 productivity","url":"https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/06/11/liquid-glass-is-more-than-skin-deep-on-macos-tahoe"},{"title":"macOS Tahoe - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MacOS_Tahoe"},{"title":"macOS Tahoe 26.1 is here to fix your biggest Liquid Glass complaint - PhoneArena","url":"https://www.phonearena.com/news/macos-tahoe-26.1-is-here-to-fix-your-biggest-liquid-glass-complaint_id175441"},{"title":"Top 7 Interface Tweaks in macOS 26 Tahoe (and How to Turn Them Off)","url":"https://mongeit.consulting/top-7-interface-tweaks-in-macos-26-taho/"},{"title":"You can reduce Liquid Glass transparency on macOS Tahoe — here's how","url":"https://www.tomsguide.com/computing/macos/you-can-reduce-liquid-glass-transparency-on-macos-tahoe-heres-how"},{"title":"Apple to refine macOS 27 with 'Liquid Glass' design tweaks after macOS 26 Tahoe backlash","url":"https://macdailynews.com/2026/05/11/apple-to-refine-macos-27-with-liquid-glass-design-tweaks-after-macos-26-tahoe-backlash/"}]

I've completed the web search for your query about macOS 26 Tahoe Liquid Glass design guidelines. Here are the key findings:

## Liquid Glass Design Overview

Liquid Glass is a new digital meta-material that dynamically bends and shapes light, announced at WWDC 2025. It is designed to behave like real glass, allowing color to peek through, and uses real-time rendering to dynamically react to movement with reflective highlights.

## Design Guidelines

Components that automatically receive Liquid Glass in macOS Tahoe include the Toolbar, Sidebar, Menu bar, Dock, Window controls, NSPopover, and Sheets. Unlike traditional blur, Liquid Glass uses "Lensing"—bending and concentrating light.

A critical design principle is: Always avoid glass on glass, as stacking Liquid Glass elements can quickly make the interface feel cluttered.

## Accessibility Considerations

Users can navigate to Settings > Accessibility > Display & Text Size and enable "Reduce Transparency" to minimize the Liquid Glass effect. macOS Tahoe 26.1 added the much-demanded ability to customize Liquid Glass, allowing users to choose either Clear or Tinted options in System Settings > Appearance.

## Reception and Implementation Issues

macOS 26 Tahoe has sparked more controversy than iOS 26, as the Liquid Glass interface hasn't adapted as well to the larger displays and different input methods of desktops and laptops. The new version has been widely criticized for poorer optimization and stability, with issues including stuttering and graphical glitches.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics

1. **Visibility of System Status** — "The design should always keep users informed about what is going on, through appropriate feedback within a reasonable amount of time."

2. **Match Between the System and the Real World** — "The design should speak the users' language. Use words, phrases, and concepts familiar to the user, rather than internal jargon."

3. **User Control and Freedom** — "Users often perform actions by mistake. They need a clearly marked 'emergency exit' to leave the unwanted action."

4. **Consistency and Standards** — "Users should not have to wonder whether different words, situations, or actions mean the same thing."

5. **Error Prevention** — "Good error messages are important, but the best designs carefully prevent problems from occurring in the first place."

6. **Recognition Rather than Recall** — "Minimize the user's memory load by making elements, actions, and options visible. The user should not have to remember information from one part of the interface to another."

7. **Flexibility and Efficiency of Use** — "Shortcuts—hidden from novice users—may speed up the interaction for the expert user so that the design can cater to both inexperienced and experienced users."

8. **Aesthetic and Minimalist Design** — "Interfaces should not contain information that is irrelevant or rarely needed."

9. **Help Users Recognize, Diagnose, and Recover from Errors** — "Error messages should be expressed in plain language (no error codes), precisely indicate the problem, and constructively suggest a solution."

10. **Help and Documentation** — "It's best if the system doesn't need any additional explanation. However, it may be necessary to provide documentation to help users understand how to complete their tasks."

## On Heuristic Evaluation Methodology

The provided content does not specify the number of evaluators required or severity rating systems for heuristic evaluation. Those details are not included in this article.

I've completed a web search for your query about macOS VoiceOver testing, keyboard navigation, and accessibility audit checklists for apps. Here are the key findings:

## Key Resources Found

**Practical Testing Guides:**
VoiceOver is one of the fastest ways for QA engineers, developers, and product teams working on a Mac to start testing real-world accessibility issues. You don't need extra software or special hardware—just turn it on and start exploring.

**Testing Approach:**
The first thing to do with VoiceOver enabled is navigate the site entirely with the Tab key, which lets you move between interactive elements like links, buttons, and form inputs, helping you test a lot of things in one pass.

**Common Issues to Check:**
VoiceOver helps you catch common problems like incorrect heading structures, unlabeled buttons, missing alt text, and broken focus order.

**Keyboard Accessibility:**
Navigate through all interactive elements using Tab and VO + arrows to move through menus, links, buttons, and form fields, verifying that VoiceOver announces each item in a clear, meaningful way.

**Apple's Official Guidance:**
Testing your app with different accessibility settings and assistive technologies like VoiceOver reveals which tasks are possible to complete, which elements are accessible, and whether navigation is clear and logical for every use case.

**GitHub Checklist Resource:**
I found a GitHub repository (apple-accessibility-skills) that includes manual verification checklists for SwiftUI, UIKit, and AppKit accessibility audits.

The search results provide comprehensive guidance on VoiceOver testing methodology, keyboard navigation validation, and accessibility audit procedures for macOS apps.

I've completed the web search for your query. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings on UI Design Review Audit Checklist and QA Process

**Design QA Definition & Purpose:**
Design QA is a specific subset focused on verifying visual fidelity: does the implemented UI match the design spec? Traditional QA testing asks "does it work?" while design QA asks "does it look and feel right?"

**Comprehensive Checklists:**
A design QA checklist should cover typography (font, size, weight, line-height), color (backgrounds, text, borders, icons), spacing (margins, padding), alignment (grid conformance), component states (hover, focus, disabled, error), responsive behavior (at least mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints), icons and images, borders and shadows

**Visual Review Process:**
Balanced pages are more pleasing to the eye, so always double-check whether design elements, including margins, paddings, and borders, look aligned. Invite colleagues or mates to participate in the quality assurance (QA) first impression test. Let them see a design prototype for a couple of minutes and after - ask them to share their feelings about it and how they would describe it.

**Cross-Functional Review:**
The most effective teams treat design QA as a cross-functional activity where designers, developers, and QA all participate.

**UI/UX Audit Scope:**
A UI/UX design audit is a systematic evaluation of a website, app, or digital platform to uncover usability issues, improve design consistency, and align experiences with user goals.

The search results provide detailed frameworks for conducting design audits, visual reviews, and QA checklists to ensure quality before handoff to development.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

The Full Guide for Sales Teams | Walnut","url":"https://www.walnut.io/blog/product-demos/whats-a-product-demo-the-full-guide-for-sales-teams/"},{"title":"The importance of creating wow moments - UXM","url":"https://www.uxforthemasses.com/creating-wow-moments/"},{"title":"Wow Moment | Glossary | ProdPad","url":"https://www.prodpad.com/glossary/wow-moment/"},{"title":"What is a Wow moment and how can you optimize your product for them?","url":"https://www.command.ai/blog/wow-moment-saas/"},{"title":"WOW Moments - 7 Examples to show how you can use them","url":"https://userguiding.com/blog/wow-moment"},{"title":"Finding your product's first wow moment","url":"https://www.appcues.com/blog/finding-your-products-first-wow-moment"},{"title":"WOW Moments in Customer Experiences - insight6","url":"https://www.insight6.com/blog/creating-wow-moments/"},{"title":"WebCoderBench: Benchmarking Web Application Generation with Comprehensive and Interpretable Evaluation Metrics","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.02430"},{"title":"Nailing the 'Wow' Moment in Enterprise Products","url":"https://www.koruux.com/blog/wow-moment-enterprise-products/"}]

I've successfully retrieved search results for your query about product demo wow moments and evaluation criteria. Here's what the search found:

## Key Insights on Wow Moments and First Impressions

**What is a Wow Moment?**
A WOW moment is where you go beyond customer expectations and requirements—the moment where you leave your target audience speechless and mesmerized by the success of your business. Wow moments resonate on a deeper, emotional level and can transform how you view a product from being a useful item to an indispensable part of your life.

**Importance in Product Demos**
The first impression is key—when you reveal your product for the first time, there's no second chance to make a first impression. Wow moments need to happen fast, ideally when users are still new to your product during onboarding, which helps keep them engaged and reduces the chances of losing interest.

**Evaluation Criteria for Wow Moments**
According to 2024 sources, evaluation criteria include:
- Gut reaction upon opening, whether it makes you want to explore further, and the overall "vibe"
- Visual "wow" factors, whether the feeling is refined or rough, and if there's a sense of design and quality
- Emotional resonance—whether the application has personality and inspires trust

**Creating Memorable UX**
Create early wow moments to shape positive first impressions and aim for lots of wow moments to repeatedly wow your users.

content.content:
# macOS Menu Bar Best Practices

I don't have access to the actual content of the Apple Developer Documentation page you referenced. The page title "The menu bar | Apple Developer Documentation" was provided, but the full documentation content wasn't included in your request.

To give you accurate, authoritative guidance on macOS menu bar best practices, I'd recommend:

## How to Access the Information

1. **Visit directly**: Go to [Apple Developer Documentation - The Menu Bar](https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/menus)
2. **Apple HIG**: Check the Human Interface Guidelines for macOS menu standards

## General macOS Menu Bar Standards (from HIG)

While I can't quote the specific document you referenced, standard macOS menu bar conventions include:

- **Standard menu order**: App Menu, File, Edit, View, Window, Help
- **App Menu**: Contains About, Preferences, Services, Hide, Quit
- **File**: New, Open, Save, Close, Print
- **Edit**: Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All
- **Common keyboard shortcuts**: Cmd+S (Save), Cmd+Z (Undo), Cmd+Q (Quit)

## Next Steps

Could you please either:
- Share the actual documentation content text, or
- Let me know specific menu bar questions you have?

This will allow me to provide detailed, accurate guidance based on the official Apple documentation.

# VoiceOver Testing Steps and Keyboard Commands for macOS

## Enabling VoiceOver

**Three activation methods:**
- Spotlight: Command + Space, type "VoiceOver," press Return
- Keyboard shortcut: Command + F5
- System Settings: Accessibility > VoiceOver toggle

## Essential Keyboard Commands

| Action | Command |
|--------|---------|
| Open Rotor | Control + Option + U |
| Navigate forward | VO + Right Arrow |
| Navigate backward | VO + Left Arrow |
| Activate element | VO + Space |

*Note: VO = Control + Option (or Caps Lock if configured)*

## Critical Testing Checklist

**Navigation Flow**
- "Can you navigate the page from top to bottom—and back up—using only the keyboard?"
- Tab order follows visual layout logically
- Focus visibility is always clear and apparent

**Content & Semantic Structure**
- Page includes an `<h1>` heading
- Heading hierarchy in correct order (no skipping levels)
- Links and buttons have unique, meaningful labels (avoid generic "Click here")
- ARIA landmarks properly labeled
- Lists use semantic HTML (`<ul>`, `<ol>`, `<dl>`)

**Images**
- Meaningful images have descriptive alt text
- Decorative images hidden with `alt=""` or appropriate roles

**Forms & Controls**
- All buttons, dropdowns, and controls activate via keyboard
- State changes announced clearly ("expanded," "collapsed")

## Rotor Feature

The Rotor is "a built-in VoiceOver feature that lets users browse by specific element types." Press Control + Option + U to open and view grouped elements like links, headings, form controls, and landmarks—enabling rapid navigation without sequential tabbing.

## Important Safari Configuration

Enable **Safari > Preferences > Advanced > "Press Tab to highlight each item on a webpage"** for proper keyboard navigation testing.

Do Heuristic Evaluation Violation Severity Ratings Correlate with Patient Safety Severity Ratings for a Native Electronic Health Record Mobile Application? - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6436963/"},{"title":"Every Expert Has A Severity Scale: Which Is Most Effective? | by Courtney Jordan | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@courtneyjordan/every-expert-has-a-severity-scale-which-is-most-effective-2eeb3e2f21ad"},{"title":"COS 436 Heuristic Evaluation Handout Evaluating Severity +","url":"https://www.cs.princeton.edu/courses/archive/spring13/cos436/handouts/Heuristics_explained.pdf"},{"title":"Severity Ratings for Usability Problems: Article by Jakob Nielsen - NN/G","url":"https://www.nngroup.com/articles/how-to-rate-the-severity-of-usability-problems/"}]

I found detailed information about Nielsen's heuristic evaluation severity rating scale. Here's what the scale consists of:

Nielsen's severity rating scale ranges from 0 to 4: 0 (not a usability problem), 1 (cosmetic problem), 2 (minor usability problem), 3 (major usability problem), and 4 (usability catastrophe).

More specifically, 0 means "I don't agree that this is a usability problem at all"; 1 is a "cosmetic problem only: need not be fixed unless extra time is available on project"; 2 is a "minor usability problem: fixing this should be given low priority"; 3 is a "major usability problem: important to fix, so should be given high priority"; and 4 is a "usability catastrophe: imperative to fix this before product can be released".

This scale helps teams prioritize which issues to fix first based on their impact on the user experience.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Official Liquid Glass Best Practices for iOS 26 & macOS Tahoe

## Core Principles

**Golden Rule:** "Liquid Glass is best reserved for the **navigation layer** that floats above the content of your app."

**Critical Constraint:** "Always avoid glass on glass. Stacking Liquid Glass elements can quickly make the interface feel cluttered."

---

## ✅ DO's

- Apply glass exclusively to navigation elements (toolbars, tab bars, sidebars, floating action buttons, sheets, popovers)
- Use `GlassEffectContainer` when grouping multiple glass elements together
- Implement `.interactive()` modifier on iOS for touch feedback (scaling, bouncing, shimmer effects)
- Reserve tinting for primary actions only using `.buttonStyle(.glassProminent)`
- Implement morphing with `glassEffectID` and shared namespaces for fluid transitions
- Use `.regular` variant as default; apply `.clear` only over media-rich content meeting all three conditions
- Trust automatic accessibility adjustments (Reduce Transparency makes glass frostier; Increase Contrast adds borders)

---

## ❌ DON'Ts

- Never apply glass to content layers (lists, cards, tables, scrollable content)
- Never place glass on top of other glass elements without `GlassEffectContainer`
- Never mix `.regular` and `.clear` variants in the same interface region
- Never tint secondary actions or non-primary UI elements
- Never apply full-screen glass backgrounds
- Never use glass without considering contrast against underlying content

---

## Accessibility (Automatic)

No additional code required. System settings automatically trigger:
- Frostier glass under Reduce Transparency
- High-contrast borders under Increase Contrast
- Disabled elastic effects under Reduce Motion

content.text: Let me confirm the NN/g severity article and get WCAG contrast + macOS Accessibility Inspector specifics.

input.prompt: What is the severity rating scale (0-4 exact definitions)? What three factors determine severity? How many evaluators does Nielsen recommend for rating severity, and why? Any guidance on how many evaluators find how many problems.

input.query: WCAG 2.2 color contrast ratio 4.5:1 3:1 text non-text Accessibility Inspector Xcode audit

content.content:
# Severity Rating Scale (0-4)

According to Nielsen's framework:

- **0**: "I don't agree that this is a usability problem at all"
- **1**: "Cosmetic problem only: need not be fixed unless extra time is available"
- **2**: "Minor usability problem: fixing this should be given low priority"
- **3**: "Major usability problem: important to fix, so should be given high priority"
- **4**: "Usability catastrophe: imperative to fix this before product can be released"

# Three Factors Determining Severity

Severity combines:
1. **Frequency** – How often the problem occurs
2. **Impact** – How difficult users find it to overcome
3. **Persistence** – Whether it's a one-time or recurring issue

The article also mentions **market impact** as a consideration, since some problems significantly affect product popularity despite being objectively easy to fix.

# Evaluator Recommendations

Nielsen recommends using "the mean of a set of ratings from three evaluators" as satisfactory for practical purposes. He notes that ratings from a single evaluator are unreliable, but quality improves rapidly as more evaluators participate.

The article doesn't provide specific guidance on how many problems individual evaluators typically identify.

Web search results for query: "WCAG 2.2 color contrast ratio 4.5:1 3:1 text non-text Accessibility Inspector Xcode audit"

Links: [{"title":"WCAG 2.2 Color Contrast: AA & AAA Requirements — Tools & Fixes Guide","url":"https://web-accessibility-checker.com/en/blog/color-contrast-checker-wcag-guide"},{"title":"Contrast requirements for WCAG 2.2 Level AA | Make Things Accessible","url":"https://www.makethingsaccessible.com/guides/contrast-requirements-for-wcag-2-2-level-aa/"},{"title":"Color Contrast for Accessibility: WCAG Guide (2026)","url":"https://www.webability.io/blog/color-contrast-for-accessibility"},{"title":"WCAG 2.2 Color Contrast — Rules & How to Comply","url":"https://rgblind.com/blog/wcag-color-contrast-requirements"},{"title":"WCAG 2 Colour Contrast Guidelines 4.5:1, 3:1 & Formula","url":"https://accessibilityassistant.com/blog/accessibility-insights/wcag-2-colour-contrast-accessibility-guidelines/"},{"title":"Understanding Success Criterion 1.4.11: Non-text Contrast | WAI | W3C","url":"https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/non-text-contrast.html"},{"title":"WebAIM: Contrast and Color Accessibility - Understanding WCAG 2 Contrast and Color Requirements","url":"https://webaim.org/articles/contrast/"},{"title":"Color Contrast Accessibility: Complete WCAG 2025 Guide + Free Checker Tool - AllAccessible Blog | AllAccessible","url":"https://www.allaccessible.org/blog/color-contrast-accessibility-wcag-guide-2025"},{"title":"Color Contrast Checker: Your Guide to WCAG 2.2 & Accessibility","url":"https://achecker.ca/contrast-checker"},{"title":"Evaluate text and background color combinations meet the 4.5:1 AA contrast ratio - AccessibilityChecker.org","url":"https://www.accessibilitychecker.org/wcag-guides/ensure-the-contrast-between-foreground-and-background-colors-meets-wcag-2-aa-minimum-contrast-ratio-thresholds/"}]

I've completed the web search for WCAG 2.2 color contrast requirements and related testing information. Here are the key findings:

## WCAG 2.2 Color Contrast Requirements

WCAG 2.2 requires 4.5:1 contrast for normal text, 3:1 for large text and UI elements. More specifically:

- Normal text needs a minimum 4.5:1 ratio, large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold+) needs 3:1, and non-text UI components (buttons, form fields, focus rings) need 3:1.
- Large-scale text and images of large-scale text have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1.

## Text vs. Non-Text Contrast

The distinction is important: Non-text Contrast (Level AA) requires the visual presentation of user interface components and graphical objects to have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s).

## Testing and Auditing Tools

For developers using Xcode and web browsers:

- Chrome and Firefox both show contrast ratios in the element inspector, and hovering over a color value shows the ratio and WCAG compliance level.
- Modern browsers and dev tools now calculate computed color contrast in the inspector, making real-time validation much easier.

Color contrast failures account for over 80% of WCAG audit issues on typical websites.

I've completed a web search for your query about Xcode Accessibility Inspector and accessibility testing for SwiftUI apps on macOS. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Xcode Accessibility Inspector Overview

The Xcode Accessibility Inspector helps developers understand how assistive technologies interact with their app's UI. To launch the Accessibility Inspector, go to Xcode > Open Developer Tool > Accessibility Inspector, and you can connect it to different devices and processes, such as the Simulator while developing or to the proper device with the app installed.

## Core Features

Using the "Target Selection" button, you can hover or tap on the element to highlight it and inspect related attributes such as the label, value, traits, and hint. You can also check and perform its actions.

In the navigation section, you can let the Accessibility Inspector mimic VoiceOver by reading the description of the selected element, navigate to the previous or next accessible element or navigate automatically through the entire app to simulate the real VoiceOver experience.

## Audit Functionality

The Accessibility Inspector also has an "Audit" feature that allows you to run an audit on your app and see what accessibility elements are missing. By default, it scans element descriptions, hit regions, contrast, element detection, parent/child relationships and actions on Mac, and on iOS, element descriptions, contrast, hit regions, element detection, clipped text, traits and large text.

## Automated Testing with Xcode 15+

In addition to performing accessibility audits with the Accessibility Inspector, developers can automate the tests in the UI Tests with the performAccessibilityAudit(for:_:), introduced in Xcode 15. You can focus tests on specific accessibility aspects like Dynamic Type by passing audit types to the method.

## Testing with VoiceOver and Dynamic Type

It's important to remember that audits are limited to the elements on the screen, and to really make sure your app is easy to use for everyone audits must be paired with user testing, testing how usable the app is with assistive technologies such as VoiceOver and applying accessibility features such as Larger Text Sizes.

Based on the search results, I found the information you're looking for:

Nielsen found that using five evaluators will suffice for the discovery of about 75% of the overall usability problems. This is a well-established finding in usability research. Between three and five evaluators seem necessary to find a reasonably high proportion of the usability problems (between 74% and 87%).

Additionally, one evaluator finds about 35% of usability problems, three evaluators find about 75%, and five evaluators find about 85%. Beyond five, with every additional evaluator, the proportion of new usability issues will be much smaller and usually not worth the extra resources.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I have enough high-quality material. Compiling findings.

input.angle: 可用性与 UIUE 评审——Nielsen 启发式、macOS 特有交互质量维度、可访问性验收方法、UI 走查 checklist 化、demo「惊艳感」评估维度
input.summary: 本角度锚定 MAformac 做 macOS 演示助手 UIUE 前置化评审时可直接落地的方法论。核心结论:(1) Nielsen 10 条启发式仍是桌面/移动/Web 通用的评审骨架,配 0-4 级 severity 打分(frequency×impact×persistence 三因素),用 3-5 名评审员即可覆盖 74%-87% 问题,单人只 ~35%,故 UIUE 走查应至少 3 人独立打分再合议。(2) macOS 有区别于移动端的一等交互维度必须专门验收:菜单栏标准菜单与顺序、系统级快捷键(Cmd-C/S/Z 等)、拖放(Mac 是拖放平台,可见内容即应可拖)、指针 hover 态(每个可交互元素都要有可见 hover)。(3) macOS 26 Tahoe 的 Liquid Glass 只用于导航层(toolbar/sidebar/菜单栏/sheet/popover),禁止 glass-on-glass、禁止给内容层加玻璃,系统会在 Reduce Transparency/Increase Contrast/Reduce Motion 下自动降级——这既是惊艳来源也是可访问性风险点,Tahoe 已因对比度/稳定性遭批评。(4) 可访问性验收有成体系工具链:VoiceOver(Cmd-F5,VO=Ctrl+Option,VO+方向键导航,Ctrl+Option+U 开 Rotor)手测焦点顺序/标签/traits/状态播报,Xcode Accessibility Inspector 的 Audit 可自动扫描 label/contrast/hit region/clipped text,Xcode 15+ 的 performAccessibilityAudit 可在 UITests 里自动化(含 Dynamic Type 维度),但自动审计仅覆盖屏上元素、必须配人工 VoiceOver+大字号测试。(5) WCAG 2.2 对比度硬门:正文 4.5:1、大字(18pt/14pt bold)3:1、非文本 UI 组件/焦点环 3:1——Liquid Glass 半透明尤其易踩。(6) demo「惊艳感」可拆成可评维度:打开瞬间的 gut reaction/vibe、视觉 wow 与精致感、情感共鸣与信任感,且 wow moment 要来得快(首因效应,onboarding 早期)。(7) UI 走查应 checklist 化,覆盖 typography/color/spacing/alignment/组件状态(hover/focus/disabled/error)/响应式,并做「首印象测试」(让人看几分钟凭感觉描述)。

findings.claim: Nielsen 10 条可用性启发式(可见系统状态/贴合真实世界/用户控制与自由/一致性与标准/防错/识别优于记忆/灵活高效/美观极简/帮助用户识别诊断并从错误恢复/帮助与文档)自1994年起30年未变,官方明言同样适用于桌面(desktop)、移动、Web,可作 SaaS/仪表盘/应用的通用评审骨架。
findings.source_title: 10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design — NN/G
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch 取到 10 条完整名称+定义原文。适合作 UIUE 走查的评审维度清单。

findings.claim: 启发式评审用 0-4 级严重度打分:0=非可用性问题,1=仅外观问题(有余力再改),2=次要(低优先级),3=主要(高优先级必改),4=灾难(发布前必须修);严重度由 frequency(频率)、impact(克服难度)、persistence(是否反复)三因素综合,外加 market impact 考量。
findings.source_title: Severity Ratings for Usability Problems — NN/G
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch 确认 0-4 定义原文与三因素。MAformac UIUE 评审可直接用此表给每条 finding 定级。

findings.claim: Nielsen 建议用「3 名评审员评分的均值」作为严重度评级的实用标准:单人评级不可靠,增加评审员质量快速提升。发现率经验值:1 人约 35%,3 人约 75%,5 人约 85%,3-5 人可覆盖 74%-87% 的可用性问题,超过 5 人边际收益递减。
findings.source_title: Severity Ratings for Usability Problems — NN/G
findings.notes: 严重度用3人均值来自WebFetch原文;35%/75%/85% 与 74-87% 来自 NN/g CHI'92 相关搜索摘要(nngroup.com/articles/usability-problems-found-by-heuristic-evaluation)。建议 UIUE 走查至少 3 人独立打分再合议。

findings.claim: macOS 是指针驱动平台:每个可交互元素都必须响应 hover/click/right-click/drag,且所有可交互元素必须有可见的 hover 悬停态——这是移动端没有、桌面评审必须专门验收的维度。
findings.source_title: macOS HIG — User Interaction / Drag and Drop
findings.notes: 来自 WebSearch 对 Apple HIG 页的摘要(HIG 页 JS 渲染 WebFetch 取不到正文)。指针 hover 态可作 macOS 专属走查项。

findings.claim: Mac 是拖放(drag-and-drop)平台:用户看得见的内容就预期能拖走,应支持重排、跨容器移动、从 Finder 导入、导出;拖动时要显示指示落点结果的指针(copy 指针/drag link/disappearing item/operation not allowed 等)。
findings.notes: WebSearch 摘要。MAformac demo 卡片若可见即应考虑可拖/或明确不可拖的指针反馈。

findings.claim: macOS 菜单栏应启用系统定义的标准菜单与菜单项(用户预期在每个 app 找到熟悉菜单),并沿用默认排序(App/File/Edit/View/Window/Help);常用菜单项要配用户已知的标准快捷键(Cmd-C 复制、Cmd-S 保存、Cmd-Z 撤销等),仅在必要时才自定义快捷键。
findings.notes: WebSearch 对 HIG 菜单栏/菜单/快捷键页的摘要(正文 JS 渲染取不到)。可作 macOS 专属验收项:标准菜单齐全+顺序+快捷键。

findings.claim: macOS 26 Tahoe 的 Liquid Glass 最佳实践:玻璃只用于漂浮在内容之上的导航层(toolbar/tab bar/sidebar/floating action button/sheet/popover),绝不给内容层(list/card/table/滚动内容)或全屏背景加玻璃;铁律是「避免 glass on glass」,多个玻璃元素要用 GlassEffectContainer 分组,默认用 .regular 变体、.clear 仅用于富媒体之上,主操作才 tint。
findings.source_title: Liquid Glass in Swift: Official Best Practices for iOS 26 & macOS Tahoe — DEV
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch 取到完整 DO/DON'T 清单。MAformac 若追求惊艳可用玻璃导航层,但要守住这些约束防止显廉价/杂乱。

findings.claim: Liquid Glass 的可访问性由系统自动降级、无需额外代码:Reduce Transparency 会让玻璃更磨砂、Increase Contrast 会加高对比边框、Reduce Motion 会禁用弹性动效;评审时应在这三种设置下验收 demo 仍可读可用。
findings.source_title: Liquid Glass in Swift: Official Best Practices — DEV
findings.notes: WebFetch 确认。演示前应切换这三项设置各走查一遍,尤其现场投影对比度不足时。

findings.claim: macOS 26 Tahoe 的 Liquid Glass 在桌面大屏与非触控输入上适配不如 iOS,遭遇较多争议:对比度/可读性投诉、卡顿与图形故障;Tahoe 26.1 才补上在系统设置里选 Clear/Tinted、降低透明度的能力(Settings>Accessibility>Display & Text Size>Reduce Transparency)。
findings.source_title: Liquid Glass on macOS Tahoe / Tahoe 26.1 Reduce Transparency 相关报道
findings.notes: 综合多条 WebSearch 摘要(appleinsider/phonearena/tomsguide/macrumors)。提示 MAformac 不要过度依赖玻璃透明,现场投影更该保守。

findings.claim: macOS VoiceOver 验收操作:Cmd-F5 开关,VO=Ctrl+Option(或 Caps Lock),VO+左右方向键顺序导航、VO+Space 激活元素、Ctrl+Option+U 打开 Rotor 按元素类型(标题/链接/表单控件/landmark)跳转;必查焦点顺序是否跟随视觉布局、焦点是否始终可见、按钮/控件是否有唯一有意义的标签、状态变化(expanded/collapsed)是否被清晰播报。
findings.source_title: How to Use VoiceOver for Accessibility Testing on macOS — Netguru
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch 取到快捷键表+Rotor+检查清单。可作 MAformac macOS VoiceOver 手测 SOP。

findings.claim: Xcode Accessibility Inspector 的 Audit 功能可自动扫描:在 Mac 上扫 element descriptions/hit regions/contrast/element detection/父子关系/actions;Xcode 15+ 新增 performAccessibilityAudit(for:_:) 可在 UITests 中自动化,并能按维度(如 Dynamic Type)聚焦;但自动审计仅覆盖当前屏上元素,必须配人工 VoiceOver 与大字号(Larger Text)测试才完整。
findings.source_title: Testing your app's accessibility with the Accessibility Inspector — Create with Swift
findings.notes: WebSearch 摘要(createwithswift/holyswift/polpiella)。MAformac 可把 performAccessibilityAudit 挂进 UITests 做机械门,人工 VoiceOver 做补充。

findings.claim: WCAG 2.2 对比度硬门:正文文字最低 4.5:1,大字(18pt 或 14pt bold 以上)3:1,非文本 UI 组件(按钮/表单/焦点环)及图形对象与相邻色最低 3:1(SC 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast);对比度失败占典型可访问性审计问题的 80% 以上,Liquid Glass 半透明尤其容易踩线。
findings.source_title: Understanding SC 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast — W3C WAI / WCAG 2.2
findings.notes: WebSearch 摘要(w3.org/webaim)。4.5:1/3:1/3:1 三档可直接作 UIUE 对比度验收阈值。

findings.claim: UI 走查/设计 QA 应 checklist 化,覆盖 typography(字体/字号/字重/行高)、color(背景/文字/边框/图标)、spacing(margin/padding)、alignment(网格对齐)、组件状态(hover/focus/disabled/error)、响应式断点、图标图片、边框阴影;设计 QA 问「看起来/感觉对不对」区别于功能 QA 问「能不能用」,最有效的团队让设计/开发/QA 跨职能共同参与。
findings.source_title: What Is Design QA? Process and Checklist (2026) — OverlayQA
findings.notes: WebSearch 摘要(overlayqa/eleken)。可直接改造成 MAformac UIUE 走查 checklist 模板。

findings.claim: demo/产品「惊艳感」可拆成可评维度:打开瞬间的 gut reaction 与整体 vibe、是否让人想继续探索、视觉 wow 与精致/质感、体感是精致还是粗糙、情感共鸣与是否让人产生信任;且 wow moment 要来得快——首因效应下「第一次揭幕没有第二次机会」,理想是在用户还新鲜的 onboarding 早期就出现。
findings.source_title: The importance of creating wow moments — UXM
findings.notes: WebSearch 摘要(uxforthemasses/koruux/appcues)。契合 MAformac 北极星『5 分钟内看着惊艳』——评审可把这几条做成 operator review 评分维度。

findings.claim: macOS 可访问性还应验收 Full Keyboard Access/键盘导航:VoiceOver 开启后先用 Tab 键从上到下再返回、只用键盘走遍所有可交互元素(链接/按钮/表单),验证 Tab 顺序跟随视觉布局逻辑、焦点可见性始终清晰、每个元素被清晰有意义地播报。
findings.source_title: VoiceOver for Mac testing steps — BBC Accessibility
findings.notes: WebSearch + netguru 交叉印证的键盘导航步骤。键盘全通路是 macOS demo 的基本可访问性门槛。
